summary: Matched several functions in src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100.c for the zako/Attack100 work. The PR body says it was ported from stephenjayakar:pr-15 (#2238) and reworked to avoid regressions to fn_800DAA40 and fn_800DAD18. The diff removes an unnecessary function-pointer cast, adjusts local declarations, uses direct user_data access with PAD_STACK in fn_800DAA40, and adds narrow wrapper calls around fn_800DAA40 and fn_800DAD18 callsites. Bot report records 4 new matches in the ftCo_Attack100 unit.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; contains all matching edits for fn_800D7938, fn_800D8EC8, fn_800DAA40, fn_800DBBF8, and ftCo_CaptureWaitLw_Phys.

what_changed.text: Added a forward declaration for static bool fn_800DAD18(Fighter_GObj* gobj).
In fn_800D7938, replaced a casted function-pointer call to it_80291F14 with a direct call it_80291F14(temp_r31->item_gobj, temp_r4).
In fn_800D8EC8, moved Vec3 bonePos earlier in the local declarations and added an unused u32 local.
In fn_800DAA40, replaced GET_FIGHTER(arg0/arg1) with direct arg0->user_data and arg1->user_data access, added PAD_STACK(8), and changed 0.0F to 0.0f.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md:fn_800DBBF8 bullet references direct user_data access

what_changed.text: Added a wrapper named fn_800DAD18_noinline that returns fn_800DAD18(gobj), and changed ftCo_CaptureWaitLw_Phys to call the wrapper.
Added a wrapper named fn_800DBBF8_noinline that calls fn_800DAA40(fighter, gobj2->victim_gobj), and changed fn_800DBBF8 to call that wrapper instead of calling fn_800DAA40 directly.
Bot report recorded 4 new matches: fn_800DBBF8, ftCo_CaptureWaitLw_Phys, fn_800D7938, and fn_800D8EC8, with +1592 matched-code bytes overall.

smart_moves.text: The PR explicitly preserved nearby callee matches while landing new matches: the body says the port was reworked to avoid regressions to fn_800DAA40 and fn_800DAD18.
Dropping the cast around it_80291F14 simplified the callsite and is associated with fn_800D7938 reaching 100% in the bot report.
The wrapper-call approach narrowed inlining/codegen effects to specific callsites rather than changing the callee bodies broadly.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a callsite has an unnecessary function-pointer cast, try the typed direct function call before preserving the cast; in this PR, removing the it_80291F14 cast contributed to matching fn_800D7938.

Inlining sensitivity can affect both the target caller and nearby callees; this PR used narrow wrapper calls to keep fn_800DAA40 and fn_800DAD18 out of line while matching fn_800DBBF8 and ftCo_CaptureWaitLw_Phys.

Small local declaration changes, including declaration order and unused stack locals, may be part of a narrow match, but should be treated as matching tactics requiring verifier evidence rather than style.

naming_conventions.text: Unknown or not-yet-semantically-named functions remain address-style fn_800Dxxxx names in this PR, consistent with conservative naming when semantics are not proven.
Wrapper names fn_800DAD18_noinline and fn_800DBBF8_noinline encode a local codegen intent rather than recovered game semantics; do not generalize them as semantic names without more evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Remove casted function-pointer call and call it_80291F14 directly.
Use direct GObj user_data loads instead of GET_FIGHTER in fn_800DAA40.
Add PAD_STACK(8) in fn_800DAA40 and ftCo_CaptureWaitLw_Phys.
Introduce small wrappers named *_noinline at sensitive callsites to influence inlining/out-of-line behavior.
Adjust local declaration order and add an unused u32 local in fn_800D8EC8 for a final tiny match delta.

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev[bot] reported matched code 72.50% (+0.04%, +1592 bytes) and 4 new matches in main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100: fn_800DBBF8, ftCo_CaptureWaitLw_Phys, fn_800D7938, and fn_800D8EC8.
No review comments or human review records are present in the loaded slice.

No human review comments are available, so reviewer intent and any requested changes cannot be inferred.

The PR body uses the wording 'dont_inline' and 'noinline wrapper'; the diff shows static inline wrappers named *_noinline. Treat the exact compiler mechanism as local PR evidence only, not a global rule.
The bot report supports match outcomes, but no objdiff logs or local build commands are included beyond the report summary.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Drop unnecessary function-pointer casts when a typed direct call matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2666, fn_800D7938 reached 100% after replacing a casted call to it_80291F14 with a direct typed call. Candidate scope should stay around ftCo_Attack100/callsites with known prototypes unless corroborated elsewhere.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use narrow wrapper calls for inlining-sensitive ftCo_Attack100 matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2666 used wrapper calls around fn_800DAA40 and fn_800DAD18 to avoid regressions while matching fn_800DBBF8 and ftCo_CaptureWaitLw_Phys. This is a narrow codegen tactic and should retain verifier evidence if reused.

source_update_candidates.text: PR #2666 changed only src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100.c and bot-reported 4 new matches in the ftCo_Attack100 unit: fn_800DBBF8, ftCo_CaptureWaitLw_Phys, fn_800D7938, and fn_800D8EC8. source_update_candidates.title: No global standard update recommended for noinline wrappers from this PR alone
source_update_candidates.text: This PR provides an example where wrappers named *_noinline helped a narrow match, but the existing matching-tactics standard already requires verifier evidence for such tactics. Do not promote wrapper insertion as a general standard from this single PR.
source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate is included as a negative/maintenance handoff: current standards appear sufficient; any update should avoid overgeneralizing a local tactic.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that direct user_data access is preferred over GET_FIGHTER globally; in this PR it was a local matching tactic.
Do not infer that PAD_STACK(8), unused locals, or *_noinline wrappers are accepted style without build/objdiff evidence for the specific function.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; no human review comments were loaded.
